title: Mission to Cilpar
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Mission |prev=*[[Battle of the Cliffs]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 1|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 1]] *[[Battle of Tandankin|Mission on Tandankin]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' (p. 159) and [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 118|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 118]] confirms that Tandankin is Luke Skywalker's last mission as Rogue Leader. Wedge Antilles later replaced Skywalker as Rogue Leader on Cilpar. *[[Second Battle of Endor]]{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} |conc=[[Nagai–Tof War]] |next=[[Mission to Mrlsst]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 5|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 5]] |image=[[File:XWRS2-FC.jpg]] |name=Mission to Cilpar |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] |objective= |date=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] (39:4 GrS)''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 1]]'' places the Mission to Cilpar one month after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places in 39:3. |place=[[Cilpar]] |result= |side1=*[[New Republic/Legends|New Loro]] |commanders2=*[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Boren Tascl]] *[[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Norquest]] *[[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Grode]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= Loro **[[Groznik]] **[[Chang]] **[[Rebel Rock]] **Others *[[Armored personnel carrier/Legends|Armored personnel carriers]] |ppl2=*[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] *[[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web heavy repeating blasters]] *[[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]] *1 [[All Terrain Advance Raider|AT-AR]] *Imperial [[Tank/Legends|Tanks]] *[[Crimson Squadron (Galactic Empire)|Crimson Squadron]] **1 modified [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] **[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] *[[Vance Rego]] |ppl3= |ppl4= |casual1=Light |casual2=Heavy |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|We come here to escort a supply convoy and find an Imperial blockade waiting for us !''"
"''I think you 're overreacting, Plourr. A dozen lousy TIE fighters! They probably don't know the war's over and they lost!|[[Isplourrdacartha Estillo|Plourr Ilo]] and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]|}} A '''mission to Cilpar''' resulted in a conflict in which the [[Cilpari Resistance]] and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] defeated the forces of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] on [[Cilpar]]. ==Background== Shortly after the creation of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] was assigned to make a food convoy run from [[Cilpar]] to [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]]. Cilpar was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] world with a [[Cilpari Resistance|resistance movement]] unaligned with the New Republic in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. A New Republic representative, [[Winter Celchu|Targeter]], had established contact with the Cilpar underground, led by [[Elscol Loro]]. ==The battle== {{Quote|He brought that fighter down with a stick!|Wedge Antilles, about [[Groznik]]|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 3|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 3]]}} Upon arrival in Cilpar airspace, the Rogues were attacked by a dozen of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]. [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] was shot down, so [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] landed to recover Janson. The rest of the squadron headed to their temporary [[Rogue Squadron Base|base]] just west of the city of [[Kiidan]]. They soon learned that the food convoy never arrived in the first place because someone tipped off the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces on the planet. [[File:E-web_Cilpar.jpg|thumb|left|200px|An [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web heavy repeating blaster]] fires on [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue squadron]] starfighters.]] Elsewhere, Tycho found Janson and they hide out in a cave. A group of underground forces led by Elscol Loro arrived at the New Republic encampment and captured the Rogues. Loro was pursued a traitor among the pilots betrayed them. The Rebels take the group to the devastated city of [[Tamarack]] to show them what [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Boren Tascl]] has done to the local populace in his hunting of Rebels. They were later attacked by Tascl's Imperial forces but managed to escape. At the same time, Tycho and Janson were surprised to meet a woman they believed at first to be [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Winter, who was Cilpar's "Targeter", convinced Tycho to make his way to the local [[Imperial garrison (Cilpar)|Imperial base]] to infiltrate their ranks as a [[TIE pilot]]. Shortly thereafter, he was forced to join the local TIE squadron in attacking the fleeing Rogues and underground. In the ensuing battle, Winter surprised the Imperials by engaging them with Tycho's X-wing that join her piloted by Tycho's [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]], shooting down many TIEs, including Tycho's one. [[File:VanceBetrayal-RS3TRO3.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Vance Rego]] betrays [[Winter Celchu|Winter]].]] Shortly after Winter's arrival at the cave, [[Vance Rego]], a member of the underground, arrived with a handful of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and took Janson and Winter (whom he believes to be Leia) prisoner. In the jungle, Tycho was rescued by the Imperials only to wind up in the same base-bound transport that Winter was in. Vance, the traitor, brought word to the Rogues and the Clipari Resistance that Princess Leia has been captured. They decide to both hit the Imperial base and save "Leia", the underground by ground and the Rogues with their X-wings. In the ensuing battle, Tycho once again flies with the Imperials, but soon turns on them. Vance was discovered as a spy and knocked by [[Groznik]], Loro's [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion. He was then devoured by a [[Ronk/Legends|ronk]] in the jungle. Groznik nearly killed Moff Tascl for the death of [[Throm Loro]], Elscol's husband, but she instead decided to take him into custody. ==Aftermath== With the local Imperials dealt a major blow, the Rogues left for Mrlsst, with Elscol as their newest squadron member. ==Appearances== *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 1|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 2|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 2]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 3|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 3]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 4|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 4]] ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']] *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/nagai11202009/index.html|text=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs|int=The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs}} *{{DB|character|winter|Winter}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW_4_ABY_2}} {{Interlang |es=Misión a Cilpar }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Cilpar, mission to}} [[Category:Battles of Cilpar]] [[Category:Missions of the Galactic Civil War]]
